text.title: Social Meta Tags and Internal Linking
text.summary: Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tag requirements, pillar-cluster internal linking structure, and keyword cannibalization fixes.

section.title: Open Graph Requirements

rule.statement: Open Graph images must be exactly 1200x630px (1.91:1 ratio) and under 8MB. Images outside these dimensions are cropped or rejected by social platforms.
rule.rationale: Facebook, LinkedIn, and most social platforms use 1200x630 as the standard preview image size. Non-conforming images display incorrectly, reducing click-through rates on shared links.

list.item: og:title: page title (max 60 chars for optimal display)
og:description: 155-160 chars, compelling summary
og:image: absolute URL, 1200x630px, under 8MB
og:image:alt: image description for accessibility
og:url: canonical URL (must match rel=canonical)
og:type: website (default) or article (for blog posts)
og:locale: en_US (or appropriate locale)

section.reference: Complete Open Graph + Twitter Card meta tag block

list.item: twitter:card: summary_large_image for articles and content
twitter:title: max 70 chars
twitter:description: max 200 chars
twitter:image: minimum 506x506px (use the 1200x630 OG image)
twitter:creator: author Twitter handle
twitter:site: brand Twitter handle

rule.statement: Every cluster page must link back to its pillar page using anchor text containing the pillar's primary keyword. Pillar pages must link to all cluster pages.
rule.rationale: The pillar-cluster model distributes link equity from high-authority pillar pages to long-tail cluster pages, improving the ranking probability of both. Bidirectional links signal topical relationship to Google.

list.item: Identify 5-10 pillar topics (comprehensive guides on broad subjects)
Create cluster pages (specific subtopics within each pillar)
Pillar links to all cluster pages with keyword-rich anchor text
Cluster pages link back to pillar with target keyword anchor
No orphan pages — every page has at least one internal link pointing to it
Maximum 3 clicks from homepage to any page

antipattern.description: Multiple pages targeting the same primary keyword (e.g., three different pages about "SDD methodology").
antipattern.reason: Cannibalization splits link equity and authority across multiple pages, weakening all of them. Google chooses which page to rank and often picks the wrong one.

list.item: Fix: 301 redirect weaker pages to the canonical page
Fix: merge similar content into one comprehensive guide
Fix: increase internal links pointing to the preferred page
Fix: use unique primary keywords for each page — no two pages share the same target
